content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed diff on feat/9ni43-materialize-beads end-to-end: BeadComment struct + BeadDetail.comments field, dep_add_with_kind, BrMutationAdapter::working_dir accessor, ExportBeads CLI subcommand, ~700-line materialize_beads implementation with idempotent existing-title matching and own-dirty-sync discipline, visibility bumps for bundle normalizers, and ten new mock-based tests. The feature is architecturally sound: strict existing-bead matching on title+type+status+labels+role, proposal_id_map-driven depends_on resolution, milestone-scope label injection, planning rationale comments, and proper sync preflight. Two material issues surfaced. First — and blocking: BrMutationAdapter::create_bead (src/adapters/br_process.rs:1343) emits `--label=<l>` per label via cmd.kv("label", ...), but `br 0.1.35` rejects that flag at the parser (`unexpected argument '--label' found; tip: a similar argument exists: '--labels'`). Verified empirically with `br create --label=alpha --dry-run`. Since materialize_beads always injects at least the `milestone:<id>` label on every root epic, workstream epic, and task bead (see materialized_bead_labels), every real invocation fails before any journal/sync effect. Mock tests pass because MockBrAdapter returns canned success responses and never invokes a real br binary. This is pre-existing adapter code (not introduced by this bead), but it blocks the new feature from functioning end-to-end against a real br. Second — a correctness edge case: when a bundle contains two proposals with identical titles (bundle validation permits this when both carry explicit bead_ids), re-export surfaces `multiple milestone-scoped beads already match title '<t>' and type '<bt>'` from find_existing_materialized_bead (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1958-1966), because the matcher keys only on normalized title and cannot disambiguate via the canonical proposal_id. This breaks idempotency for a legitimate bundle shape. Dependency kind serialization, list_all pagination, comment restoration on reused beads, and own-pending-sync replay on retry all look correct. No other material defects found in this diff.

## Amendments

1. BrMutationAdapter::create_bead in src/adapters/br_process.rs:1333-1360 builds `--label=<value>` for each label via `cmd.kv("label", label.as_str())` (line 1343). `br 0.1.35` rejects that argument at the parser: running `br create --label=alpha --dry-run` returns `error: unexpected argument '--label' found; tip: a similar argument exists: '--labels'`. The correct invocations are either `--labels=a,b,c` (comma-separated) or `-l a -l b -l c` (repeated short flag). Because the new materialize_beads path in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs always injects at least a `milestone:<id>` label on every created bead (see materialized_bead_labels), every real-world run of `milestone export-beads <id>` fails at the first create_bead call — no beads created, no dependencies wired, no journal event emitted. The mock-based tests in service.rs don't catch this because MockBrAdapter short-circuits the subprocess entirely. Fix options: (a) change `cmd.kv("label", ...)` in a new loop to `cmd.flag_value("-l", label.as_str())` or similar, emitting `-l <label>` per label; or (b) collect labels and emit a single `--labels=<a>,<b>,<c>` key-value (being careful to forbid commas inside any individual label). Option (a) preserves the existing one-flag-per-label shape; option (b) matches br's native preferred form. Either resolves the blocker. The existing internal test at br_process.rs:2231-2232 should also be updated to assert the new flag shape.
   Rationale: Without this fix the newly landed `materialize_beads` feature and `milestone export-beads` CLI cannot complete a single bead creation against a real br 0.1.35, even though the bead's own logic is correct. The symptom would be a hard failure on the very first create_bead call, wasting adapter_id pending-sync state and confusing operators. Because the regression is in pre-existing adapter code rather than in materialize_beads itself, routing this to a follow-up bead keeps the current PR's scope clean (ADDITIVE-only) while making the blocker visible and actionable. Priority should be critical: the feature being merged is non-functional end-to-end until this is resolved.
2. find_existing_materialized_bead in src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1940-1982 keys existing beads only on normalized title. When a bundle contains two proposals with identical titles (bundle validation permits duplicate titles when both carry explicit bead_ids — see bundle validation logic and canonical_export_proposal_id), a re-export sees two milestone-scoped beads with the same title+type and bails with `multiple milestone-scoped beads already match title '<t>' and type '<bt>'` at line 1963. This silently breaks the idempotency guarantee for a legitimate bundle shape: the first export succeeds, creates two beads with identical titles and identical `milestone:<id>` labels but distinct br-assigned IDs; a subsequent export (e.g. after a crash between dep resolution passes, or a deliberate retry) cannot reconcile which existing bead corresponds to which proposal. The matcher would need to consult the canonical proposal_id (via comment body, a dedicated label like `milestone-proposal:<canonical_id>`, or a stable description marker) to disambiguate. Until fixed, bundle authors must avoid same-title proposals, which isn't enforced at validation time.
   Rationale: This edge case defeats the feature's advertised idempotency for a bundle shape that bundle validation currently permits. It is not a showstopper because in practice workstreams rarely need same-title tasks, but the failure mode is silent at validation time and noisy only on re-export, which is exactly when idempotency matters. Medium severity: real correctness gap, narrow trigger, clear mitigation paths; does not block the initial landing of this bead but should be resolved before operators rely on idempotent re-runs in production.
